title: ABC Television Network
content:

{{RealCompany
|image=[[File:ABC.svg]]
|name=ABC Television Network
|founded=[[May 13]], [[1940s|1943]]<ref name="Radio Encyclo">Sterling, Christopher (2004). The Museum of Broadcast Communications Encyclopedia of Radio. New York: Taylor & Francis. {{ISBN|1-57958-249-4}}.</ref>

The '''ABC Television Network''' is one of the major television networks in the United States. Through [[Wikipedia:Walt Disney Television|Walt Disney Television]] (former Disney–ABC Television Group), it is a subsidiary of [[The Walt Disney Company]], since [[1996]].<ref name="Disney Encyclo" /> (The American Broadcasting Company should not be confused with the [[Wikipedia:Australian Broadcasting Corporation|Australian Broadcasting Corporation]], the national broadcaster of Australia. The Australian ABC did broadcast ''[[The Star Wars Holiday Special]]'' in Australia.)

==History==
ABC's first brush with ''[[Star Wars]]'' came in [[1977]], with the airing of the one hour special ''[[The Making of Star Wars]]''.<ref name="The Making of Star Wars">{{WebCite|url=http://popwatch.ew.com/2012/02/09/star-wars-movie-magic/|text=How 'Star Wars' changed my life: George Lucas turned me into a know-it-all who can't believe in movie magic. Oh, and an entertainment journalist.|author=Jenson, Jeff|date=2012-02-09|archivedate=20120211140857}}</ref><ref name="YBY">''[[Star Wars Year By Year: A Visual History, New Edition]]''</ref> ABC then broadcast the two [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] telefilms, ''[[Caravan of Courage: An Ewok Adventure]]'' ([[1984]]) and ''[[Ewoks: The Battle for Endor]]'' ([[1985]]),<ref name="Ewok">{{SWArchive|url=eu/tv/classic/f20000824/index.html|text=Ewok Adventures: A Television Adventure}}</ref> and from 1985 to [[1986]] it broadcast the animated series [[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO|''Star Wars: Droids'']] and [[Ewoks (television series)|''Star Wars: Ewoks'']].<ref name="Insider27">{{InsiderCite|27|A Star Wars CELibration|A ''Star Wars'' CELibration}}</ref> In [[2014]], ABC hosted a special re-airing of ''[[Star Wars Rebels: Spark of Rebellion]]''.<ref name="Insider 156">{{InsiderCite|156|Star Wars Rebels Recounted|''Star Wars Rebels'' Recounted}}</ref>

In the early 1990s, [[Bob Iger (chief executive officer)|Bob Iger]] (then-chairman of ABC) gave the greenlight to [[George Lucas]]'s series [[Wikipedia:The Young Indiana Jones Chronicles|''The Young Indiana Jones Chronicles'']], which aired on the network from [[1992]] to [[1993]]. In [[2005]], Iger became CEO of Disney, and it was he who negotiated with Lucas for the company's [[2012]] acquisition of [[Lucasfilm]].<ref>{{WebCite|url=http://www.forcematerial.com/home/2016/12/5/when-you-wish-upon-a-death-star-the-disney-lucasfilm-connection|text=When You Wish Upon A Death Star: The Disney-Lucasfilm Connection|author=Williams, Rohan|date=2016-12-05|archivedate=20190818162800}}</ref>

In [[2016]], the network debuted the first two trailers for ''[[Rogue One: A Star Wars Story]]''.<ref name="Teaser preview">{{SWYouTube|MaYv3Y8tyoQ|ROGUE ONE: A STAR WARS STORY Teaser Preview}}</ref><ref>{{WebCite|url=http://makingstarwars.net/2016/07/rogue-one-a-star-wars-story-trailer-to-air-on-abc-july-15th|text=Rogue One: A Star Wars Story trailer to air on ABC on July 15th|author=Ward, Jason|date=2016-07-05|archivedate=20200108110733}}</ref>

In [[2020]], a special look for [[The Mandalorian Season Two|season two]] of ''[[Star Wars: The Mandalorian]]'' debuted on ''[[Wikipedia:Monday Night Football|Monday Night Football]]'', which was broadcast simultaneously on [[Wikipedia:ESPN|ESPN]] and on ABC.<ref name="Football">{{Twitter|disneyplus|status/1317480488567668737|[[Disney+]]|quote=Don't miss an exclusive look at the new season of #TheMandalorian during @ESPN Monday Night Football! New episodes of the Original Series start streaming Oct. 30 on #DisneyPlus.|archivedate=20201018040032}}</ref>
